metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_2684
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig. 5: In this study, Rizzoli et al show, using an in-vitro fusion assay, that the ATP-dependent fusion between neuronal endocytic vesicles and PC12-derived acceptor membranes is inhibited by recombinant endosomal proteins, such as syntaxin 6, syntaxin 13 and VAMP4. To further prove the specific involvement of endosomal SNAREs in the fusion reaction, the authors incubate neuronal and PC12 organelles with bacterial toxins (botulinum C1 and tetanus toxin), which cleave exocytotic SNAREs (synaptobrevin 2 and syntaxin 1, verified by Western blotting), but do not inhibit the reaction.
Thus, the results suggest that syntaxin 6, syntaxin 13 and vamp4 are the endosomal SNAREs required for the fusion of SVs to early endosomes.. Experimental description: Neuronal organelles used int he study were prepared fro rat brains, as stated in M&M:
Synaptic vesicles were prepared as described (21). Synaptosomes were prepared from adult rats by discontinuous Ficoll gradient centrifugation (44).

-Species origin of syntaxin-13 is rat (traced back to PMID:11101518): "Rat syntaxin 12/13 was amplified using primers (gctctagatgtcctacggtcccttagac and gcggtacctcacttagaagcaacccagataac)"
